239437.explanation: More than 20% of our residents are younger than 18 years. In addition to the Children's Council for primary school children, we also want to hear the voice of the older youth, for example through a newly established Youth Council. After all, they are the future and their ideas are often extraordinarily refreshing!

239439.explanation: The municipality must be easily accessible to residents. Therefore, we want to invest in a more user-friendly website, a digital app for notifications in public spaces among other things, and a fixed entrepreneurs’ desk. The municipal counter should also be open without appointment at least one half-day per week.

239441.explanation: Heritage must not be lost! It should therefore be better protected against demolition. This also applies to all pre-war houses and buildings in our municipality. Furthermore, the designation of municipal monuments should be faster and more proactive.

239443.explanation: If it is up to GDP, parking in Muiderberg will remain free. This way, the brink village remains hospitable and attractive to visitors. If parking regulation is necessary, it should only be implemented with sufficient support from residents and entrepreneurs.

239444.explanation: The best ideas are found on the street! GDP is therefore looking for new ways to better involve residents in important decisions. This can include an idea competition, a participatory budget, citizen initiatives, a citizen's assembly, or a local advisory referendum. Listen. Think. Act.

239445.explanation: GDP does not want property tax and parking fees to increase beyond what is currently included in the municipal budget (except for inflation correction). If additional funds are needed, the municipality should first look for them in a more efficient municipal apparatus and by cutting existing expenses.

239446.explanation: No fees will be charged for small-scale non-commercial events and the municipality will take a facilitating role. This way, we ensure that volunteers continue to enjoy organizing local activities as part of our cultural identity and that this remains financially feasible.

239447.explanation: It is increasingly difficult to find a home. Residents who are attached to the municipality and want to continue living there will therefore be given priority for allocation. Furthermore, we encourage the construction of sufficient social and mid-range (rental) housing and ensure better housing flow.

239448.explanation: Much is already being done for the integration of newcomers, with all associated costs. If more money is needed, it must come from the national government. We now set other priorities in the municipal budget; there is no room for this anymore.

239449.explanation: Many locations have already been suggested recently. Considering the recent negative experiences with the COA, we see no space for a new asylum seeker center or emergency shelter in the near future. The existing emergency shelter ('White Tower') in Naarden will be closed after the agreed two-year period.

239450.explanation: GDP wants to preserve green areas and trees in every neighborhood as much as possible and expand where necessary. This must be reviewed per area and per year (customized approach) and cannot be solved simply by setting a quota for planted trees. However, there must be better policy regarding felling permits.

239451.explanation: GDP supports the pursuit of clean air and believes proactive management based on air quality measurements is necessary. However, we consider banning wood burning excessive. Residents have their own responsibility here, and the 'burning guide' from the RIVM is sufficient.

239453.explanation: Traditions must evolve with the times. Therefore, in the event of a total fireworks ban during New Year’s Eve, we want to organize central fireworks and drone shows in every residential area together with residents.

239456.explanation: GDP wants to develop the library in Bussum together with the Wilhelmina Church into a cultural hotspot where residents (including youth) can intellectually enrich and entertain themselves with many fun, creative, and educational activities. Music and theater can also have a central place here.

239459.explanation: Safety on the streets is a basic need but is not always guaranteed. Therefore, we want to introduce preventive camera surveillance and smart street lighting at locations where there is a lot of nuisance. We will also establish reporting points, cooperate well with neighborhood watch, and ensure safe cycling routes.

239460.explanation: Traffic safety is important! For this reason, GDP wants stricter enforcement of traffic rules and to establish a maximum speed of 30 km/h within built-up areas. Cars are guests in bicycle streets and fatbikes must also be considerate of other traffic. We will actively monitor this.

239462.explanation: People like to live close to their work. To maintain our facilities, it is important to help certain target groups find housing. Think of teachers, healthcare workers, police officers, and firefighters. Together, we combat staff shortages.

239463.explanation: Due to the housing shortage, we encourage the construction of sufficient social and mid-range (rental) housing and ensure better housing flow. Because there is also a need for homes in other segments, we build at least one-third social housing and prevent housing corporations from selling rental homes.

239464.explanation: GDP supports that residents receiving welfare benefits who are not in a reintegration program should, if possible, contribute to society in other ways, such as through volunteer work. Those who cannot do this may qualify for an exemption.

239466.explanation: GDP considers a total ban on polluting vehicles too extreme. However, we do encourage emission-free buses and trucks in our centers to keep the air clean and the area livable. Heavy truck traffic will be minimized as much as possible. Air quality will be continuously monitored through measurement stations.

239468.explanation: If it is up to GDP, parking in Naarden Vesting will remain available and free for everyone. This keeps the fortress town hospitable and attractive to visitors. If parking regulation is necessary, it should only be implemented with sufficient support from residents and entrepreneurs.

239469.explanation: Traffic safety is a top priority for GDP. Therefore, we want to reduce the speed limit in built-up areas to 30 km/h and pay extra attention to traffic safety near schools, daycare centers, sports clubs, and crossings. Additionally, we will increase enforcement of traffic rules.

239470.explanation: GDP is critical of heat extraction through aquathermy. Without support and given disproportionate financial risks as we see now, we do not support continuing the heating network in Muiderberg. Moreover, this costly method does not offer a solution for other residential areas.

239471.explanation: We want the closed and evacuated petting zoos and deer parks in Bussum and Naarden to return for educational purposes and for the entertainment of young and old. The petting zoos and deer parks should be designed in an animal-friendly way and besides deer, other animals may also be kept.

239472.explanation: Wind turbines are landscape pollution and not suitable near residential areas or in the beautiful nature areas of Gooise Meren. However, we are open to research into alternative energy generation options. We encourage initiatives to realize 'neighborhood batteries'.

239473.explanation: The noise nuisance from the A1 and A6 highways must be reduced for our residents. The development of a (solar) noise barrier initiated by the neighborhood near the Naarderwoonbos can contribute to this, with attention also to noise in other neighborhoods near the highway (such as the Keverdijk).

239474.explanation: Our streets must be safe, especially in residential areas and near schools. GDP therefore supports car-reduced centers and a maximum speed of 30 km/h throughout the entire built-up area. Package delivery points increase (and often speed up) traffic and are therefore undesirable in residential areas.

239476.explanation: Gooise Meren is crossed by railways, highways, high-voltage lines, and flight paths, affecting air quality, odor, and noise nuisance. The municipality must proactively lobby to reduce this nuisance, although it does not have sole responsibility for this.

239479.explanation: We believe in equal opportunities for all children. Therefore, we maintain primary schools in all residential areas and invest in our school buildings. For young children (toddlers) with learning delays, the municipality provides good support through early and preschool education.

239480.explanation: GDP wants to accelerate the construction of an inner harbor for small boats in Muiden and near Naarden Vesting. Residents can dock their boats here and it stimulates the local economy. So: no talk, just dig!

239481.explanation: GDP finds it important to preserve green and trees in every neighborhood, including the Vijverpark in Bussum-South. In the revitalization of the district, we only want to allow new buildings on the higher elevated parts. We also want to protect other parks and create better policies for felling permits.

239482.explanation: GDP supports enough housing, better distributed – not only mid-range rental and owner-occupied homes. Sufficient attention must also be paid to the lowest incomes. Therefore, we ensure enough social and mid-range (rental) housing in new projects, both in number and living area.

239483.explanation: Agricultural land is primarily intended for farming and is very scarce in Gooise Meren. GDP does not want to build everything up easily, especially considering the nature reserves that surround us. We also stay away from the historic shooting ranges near Naarden Vesting!

239484.explanation: Owning a pet is basically a personal choice. Residents are primarily responsible for the costs that come with it. Only in exceptional cases can residents appeal to the municipality within the existing Social Support Act (Wmo) regulations.

239486.explanation: Where it is feasible, GDP advocates for more underground parking facilities. This creates more space for greenery and recreational areas. Therefore, we are exploring the possibility of a parking garage beneath the Wilhelminaplantsoen in Bussum (with a hospitality square with terraces above).

239487.explanation: Residents must be able to get the support they need; this should not be cut dramatically. However, we must closely monitor that help reaches only where it is truly necessary and that it is organized efficiently. We offer support as much as possible at the neighborhood level.

239488.explanation: GDP wants to realize one accessible care and welfare desk, both digitally and physically, with one contact person per client. There will be more cooperation between care organizations, municipality, and volunteers. We will address wait times in the Social Support Act (Wmo) and youth care regionally: less bureaucracy and faster help.

239489.explanation: Care must always be close by. GDP wants to commit to facilitating sufficient primary healthcare and easily accessible emergency care/GP care in every residential area, either consolidated in a central health center or otherwise.

239491.explanation: We encourage small-scale events, which often relate to local traditions and serve the community. Permit application procedures should be faster and simpler, and we do not charge fees for non-commercial events. Events for the youth (including nightlife events) are also supported.

239492.explanation: Our UNESCO World Heritage status proves how special cultural gems like Naarden Fortress and Muiderslot Castle are. GDP therefore wants to boost city promotion and improve accessibility to attractions in Gooise Meren. This is aimed at increasing (quality) tourism, while maintaining livability.

239437.explanation: For D66, youth have the future, so it is very important that their voices are heard. This increases their involvement and leads to better-considered decisions by the council.

239439.explanation: D66 believes that a clean and safe living environment is a core task of the municipality. Through an app, residents can more easily and quickly report issues, enabling the municipality to act purposefully. In addition to this app, personal contact with the municipality must always remain possible.

239441.explanation: D66 believes these houses should not be demolished without a well-considered choice and assessment of whether renovation might be a better alternative. The historical character of our municipality makes it unique, so it is important to handle it carefully.

239443.explanation: Paid parking is only needed to solve a possible parking problem. There is currently enough parking space in Muiderberg.

239444.explanation: D66 considers it important to involve residents in important decisions. However, topics must be suitable for this so that residents can make an informed choice based on sufficient information.

239445.explanation: D66 stands for low taxes and believes the municipality should strive for a balanced multi-year budget, always first reviewing its own expenses before burdening residents.

239446.explanation: D66 believes that higher prices for event permits should not be an obstacle for small, local events.

239447.explanation: The municipality has a national legal housing obligation. D66 believes every municipality must take responsibility to meet this legal requirement.

239448.explanation: D66 wants to ensure funds are well spent. For example, combining language and work can help newcomers integrate faster and better. Effectiveness is paramount for D66.

239449.explanation: The municipality has a legal obligation to shelter asylum seekers. D66 believes it is humane to take responsibility for accommodating people fleeing war and political violence.

239450.explanation: D66 wants Gooise Meren to climb the national green index ladder. By adding as many trees as possible, we contribute to reducing heat stress and the effects of climate change. More greenery improves the mental and physical health of our residents.

239451.explanation: For D66, the health of our residents comes first. The national 'stookwijzer' (burning guide) can be used to reduce nuisance. D66 wants to make its use mandatory for wood burning appliances. We also want to explore applying smoke filters.

239453.explanation: D66 believes organizing a fireworks show is not primarily a municipal task. If a (neighborhood) association wants to organize a fireworks show, the municipality can take a facilitating role to ensure a safe environment.

239456.explanation: Maintaining a library in every community is a priority for D66. Expanding or merging other cultural activities (lectures, exhibitions, etc.) with the library can be a great addition but must not obstruct the core role of the libraries in Gooise Meren.

239459.explanation: Permanent camera surveillance is an invasion of our privacy, with little regulation regarding the storage of footage. Occasional use around events is not excluded, as it can increase safety.

239460.explanation: D66 is concerned about the nuisance caused by fatbikes. They are often modified, creating unsafe situations among traffic participants. D66 wants more enforcement and a helmet requirement. Additionally, we want to focus more on prevention and raising awareness of the risks for young people.

239462.explanation: This policy already exists in Gooise Meren. D66 supports providing housing to professions that guarantee vital services. This ensures safety, healthcare, and education for our municipality.

239463.explanation: D66 supports the established housing vision, which states that one-third of new homes must be social rental housing. D66 primarily focuses on affordable owner-occupied and mid-rent homes for starters, families, and other middle incomes to stimulate housing flow.

239464.explanation: D66 believes everyone who truly needs it should qualify for social assistance benefits without immediate reciprocity. D66 strongly supports the principle of solidarity, which is the foundation of our welfare state.

239466.explanation: D66 considers clean air extremely important. At the same time, the strict requirements of environmental zones are still difficult for small municipalities to enforce.

239468.explanation: In Naarden Fortress, there is a shortage of parking spaces for residents. By combining paid parking with resident parking permits, we ensure a parking space for everyone in the limited space.

239469.explanation: D66 wants a less prominent role for cars in our communities, where reducing speed promotes more equality with other road users. An exception for us are the through roads that should facilitate traffic flow.

239470.explanation: D66 strongly supports the Muiderberg district heating network started by a residents' initiative. This will make an important contribution to the regional energy transition, which our municipality has also committed to. D66 believes in encouraging residents' initiatives.

239471.explanation: D66 considers keeping deer behind fences animal cruelty and outdated. We value nature education and therefore support transforming deer parks into animal enclosures or petting farms.

239472.explanation: D66 is a strong advocate for the energy transition and believes every municipality must take responsibility. There is an opportunity to place wind turbines at Diemerscheg. Adequate distance from homes is always a condition.

239473.explanation: Noise nuisance is very harmful to the health of our residents. D66 supports this residents' initiative. Additionally, this project offers a chance to contribute to the energy transition via solar panels while simultaneously boosting biodiversity along the A1 motorway.

239474.explanation: Parcel pickup points cause a lot of nuisance when located in the middle of residential neighborhoods. Think of noise pollution and dangerous situations, especially for children. D66 believes we should focus on locating parcel pickup points at neighborhood edges to keep our streets safe for children.

239476.explanation: For D66, the health of our residents takes precedence over air traffic, especially at night. We therefore support the work of groups such as the noise nuisance group 'Minder Hinder' that advocate for this.

239479.explanation: D66 wants all children to have equal opportunities, regardless of where they were born. Childcare benefits child development. Equal opportunity starts with the very youngest children. For parents who cannot afford childcare, it should be accessible and therefore free.

239480.explanation: Many of our residents enjoy our water-rich municipality. D66 believes we should continue to enable this where possible. However, plans must be feasible and affordable.

239481.explanation: There is also a housing shortage in Gooise Meren. D66 sees opportunities to redesign the neighborhood with plenty of greenery, walking paths, thereby strengthening nature and livability in the area.

239482.explanation: D66 mainly wants to focus on affordable owner-occupied and mid-rent homes for starters, families, and other middle incomes to stimulate housing flow. Additionally, D66 supports the housing vision which sets that one-third of new-build homes must be social rental housing.

239483.explanation: There is also a housing shortage in Gooise Meren. D66 believes that in these times we cannot afford to reject land suitable for housing development in advance.

239484.explanation: D66 does not see this as a core task of the municipality. Fortunately, there are private aid organizations that can support residents in such situations.

239486.explanation: D66 favors removing cars from the streets and greening centers. However, this must be financially feasible, garages must largely be self-sufficient, and it must not come at the expense of the municipality's level of services.

239487.explanation: Cutting budgets for the Social Support Act (Wmo) often leads to higher healthcare costs later. For example, residents may avoid care (and costs) which worsens health complaints. Cheap thus becomes expensive.

239488.explanation: Requesting and arranging care can be very complicated. D66 believes residents must always be able to address care-related questions at one fixed location.

239489.explanation: D66 believes that healthcare should always be organized close to the residents. We strive to have a health center in every neighborhood as much as possible.

239491.explanation: D66 considers vibrant town centers very important, with events being organized as easily accessible as possible for our residents and entrepreneurs.

239492.explanation: The liveliness in our centers is driven by residents and entrepreneurs. More liveliness is good for our local economy and thus for our municipality.

239437.explanation: According to the VVD, everyone should be able to participate. At the same time, we see that some target groups are not sufficiently reached. A youth council can play an important role in involving young people in municipal plans.

239439.explanation: The VVD believes that reports should be easy and accessible. An app can help with this. The municipality offers digital support wherever possible and personal help where necessary, for example for elderly people who are less digitally skilled. This way, everyone can choose the form that suits them best.

239441.explanation: The VVD facilitates good plans. Sometimes this can also mean that if pre-war houses can be renewed or improved, demolition may apply. Not everything built before the war is automatically worthy of protection.

239443.explanation: Parking in Muiderberg is already free and, according to the VVD, should remain so. There is no reason to introduce paid parking.

239444.explanation: The VVD values residents’ involvement, especially at the beginning of the decision-making process. Early participation offers more room for dialogue and influence, while a referendum usually takes place only at the end and is limited to a yes-or-no choice on complex matters.

239445.explanation: Raising property tax (ozb), parking fees, and other charges further is not what Gooise Meren needs now according to the VVD. These municipal levies may only be increased by the inflation correction at most.

239446.explanation: The VVD supports the organization of events. These often arise from local initiatives and usually with a modest budget. The municipality should not charge high fees for permits that make initiatives possible.

239447.explanation: In anticipation of legislation, the VVD believes we should address this within the municipality. When allocating housing, it is important for the VVD that housing corporations do not all buy up houses in the same neighborhoods or streets to house status holders, thereby 'disrupting' social cohesion in a neighborhood or street.

239448.explanation: Integration is not optional. Newcomers must participate from day one, learn the language, and give something back to society. Social organizations support this and deserve the municipality’s support.

239449.explanation: It has been agreed that the emergency shelter in Naarden will remain open for a maximum of two years. As far as the VVD is concerned, this should stay that way and no new (emergency) shelters will open after closure.

239450.explanation: The VVD wants to plant at least 1,000 new trees in the coming years. This creates climate-resilient neighborhoods that make Gooise Meren greener and a more pleasant place to live.

239451.explanation: The coziness and warmth of a wood stove is the main reason many people buy one. Modern wood stoves are made with attention to quality, environment, and sustainability. People can sensibly choose to turn off the stove in bad weather. The municipality does not need to be involved in this.

239453.explanation: A locally organized fireworks show that suits each center deserves the VVD's preference, especially now that there is a national fireworks ban.

239456.explanation: The VVD Gooise Meren considers the library an important meeting place in the municipality. We support the library in finding future-proof housing and offering a broad, accessible program.

239459.explanation: According to the VVD, camera surveillance should be present where necessary. It increases the chance of catching offenders and provides monitoring of risk areas. We will actively engage with the Public Prosecution Service to ensure this is facilitated.

239460.explanation: The VVD will address nuisance caused by fatbikes. We are interested in bans as implemented in other municipalities and do not rule out (similar) measures in our own municipality.

239462.explanation: These professions are essential to our society and should be given priority to continue living in our municipality wherever possible.

239463.explanation: For the VVD, a fixed percentage is not an end in itself. We do not just build houses; we work on strong and balanced communities. We see a great shortage of suitable homes for starters and elderly people with a middle income.

239464.explanation: The VVD finds work meaningful and important because participation in society contributes to self-esteem and independence. We think it is important that people continue to participate where possible. At the same time, we recognize that not everyone receiving social assistance can work.

239466.explanation: There are various ways to tackle polluting vehicles. As far as the VVD is concerned, environmental zones do not need to be introduced in our municipality.

239468.explanation: Permit parking is not a goal in itself for the VVD. It can be introduced if it contributes to accessibility and traffic safety and has sufficient support.

239469.explanation: Almost all roads within the built-up area are already 30 km/h throughout the municipality. A few roads are not yet 30 km/h. According to the VVD, these should also be reduced to 30 km/h to make an additional contribution to traffic safety.

239470.explanation: Continuing the heat network is not an end in itself. The VVD attaches clear conditions to it. Using the heat network as a promising solution for sustainable heating requires good communication, clear progress, and a solid financial basis.

239471.explanation: The deer enclosures will return as animal meadows where animals play a prominent role again. These animal meadows are important meeting and educational places for young and old.

239472.explanation: The VVD consciously chooses solutions that fit the character of our municipality. We consider preserving nature, such as our forests, very important. Therefore, we focus on solar energy on roofs and via solar carports, not on large wind turbines.

239473.explanation: Since the beginning, the VVD has supported the residents’ initiative for a solar noise barrier at the Naarderwoonbos. This is important to enhance the livability of the neighborhood, with attention to possible effects on Keverdijk, such as sound reflection.

239474.explanation: Package points provide convenience and simplicity, but an industrial estate is a more logical location for this. According to the VVD, this should not cause nuisance in the middle of residential neighborhoods due to extra traffic and parking problems.

239476.explanation: Last year a position paper was drafted advocating this. The VVD supports active lobbying on this matter. Nighttime air traffic negatively affects the health and sleep of our residents.

239479.explanation: The VVD stands for an equal start for every child. We prevent language and developmental delays by working together with social organizations like the library, preschools, and childcare.

239480.explanation: The VVD invests in facilities enabling recreation on and around the water, with attention to nature and safety. According to the VVD, this can be done at Fort-H in Muiden and in Naarden-Vesting.

239481.explanation: Where possible, we add affordable homes fitting the environment of Vijverpark while preserving greenery and water, with ongoing attention to good traffic flow and suitable parking solutions. Housing construction is only possible once traffic flow is properly arranged.

239482.explanation: The VVD wants more attention for the middle segment. Currently, too few homes are built for people with a modal and middle income. This is good for the housing market’s fluidity.

239483.explanation: To build on agricultural land, permission from the Province is required. The VVD focuses on this if building in the existing environment is no longer possible.

239484.explanation: Residents who get a pet are personally responsible for the care and costs. According to the VVD, the municipality should not bear these costs.

239486.explanation: Underground parking garages cost tens of millions each. That is money the municipality does not have. Parking must remain affordable. According to the VVD, there should be enough safe parking spaces, but not all can be underground.

239487.explanation: The VVD wants to manage resources for social support more carefully and purposefully, looking at what fits the requests and needs of our residents. The VVD wants to help residents with Wmo resources to live as long as possible in their own familiar environment.

239488.explanation: Care issues are often complex and residents are sometimes passed from pillar to post. That is why the VVD wants one single counter in each center where everyone can go for an overview and quick support.

239489.explanation: The VVD believes that every neighborhood should have its own health center. This way, residents have quick and clear access to care close to home and can live independently at home for longer. Accessible and well-organized care nearby is the core principle.

239491.explanation: The VVD wants permits to be simpler and faster granted through a single (digital) counter, with clear processing times and lower thresholds for small-scale initiatives.

239492.explanation: Cultural heritage should be cherished, according to the VVD. By attracting tourists, we spotlight these gems. We actively promote local traditions and heritage (including fortified towns, the Dutch Waterline, and various museums) and link them to events and quality tourism.

239437.explanation: The municipality should involve young people more often. This doesn't have to be only through a youth council. We want to actively engage young people and schools through class visits, pizza sessions, workshops and the residents’ panel. This way, more young people gain real influence on plans affecting their future.

239439.explanation: If something is broken or dangerous in your neighborhood, reporting it should be quick and easy: via an app, an accessible website, and by phone. It's important that the municipality responds quickly and truly resolves the problem. Together, we keep the neighborhood clean, safe, and pleasant.

239441.explanation: We want enough affordable and well-insulated homes. This can be challenging with old houses of historical value. Per neighborhood, after consulting residents, we choose between renovation or new construction, and thus demolition if necessary. We carefully weigh the interests between heritage, housing needs, and sustainability.

239443.explanation: Paid parking is only necessary if scarce parking spaces remain occupied for a long time and if accessibility and livability are at risk due to parking pressure. Fortunately, this hardly occurs in Muiderberg. Therefore, paid parking is not needed here.

239444.explanation: Residents should be able to think along earlier and better. This can be done via panels, neighborhood conversations and experts. But with building plans, things often go wrong now. A referendum is too limited; it only allows for a Yes or No answer. The council works well only when decisions are broadly supported. This must improve significantly.

239445.explanation: Good care, accessible facilities, and strong implementation are important to us. We do not want to cut back on these. Therefore, if necessary, we prefer a higher property tax (ozb). This ensures those who can afford it pay more and keeps our municipality a place where everyone can participate.

239446.explanation: Culture is important for everyone: events bring people together and make our municipality lively. Permit fees must remain cost-covering, but if organizing becomes too expensive, subsidies should be easy to obtain and low-threshold so that events remain accessible for all of us.

239447.explanation: Everyone needs a safe home. Status holders do not receive extras; the municipality must help them if they are allowed to stay here. The real problem is the housing shortage. Therefore, we build more affordable houses for everyone and ensure that status holders can live well distributed throughout the municipality.

239448.explanation: It is smart for the municipality to spend more money on integration. When newcomers quickly learn the language and can participate in school, work and the neighborhood, things improve for everyone. This prevents problems later and helps people contribute to building our society.

239449.explanation: Gooise Meren must provide 389 places for asylum seekers but currently has 150. Therefore, we must already look for new places now and not wait until Naarden closes. We want to be solidary and take our responsibility. The shelters must be humane and small scale, with good communication with the neighborhood.

239450.explanation: Trees make neighborhoods cooler and healthier for people and animals. They help against heat and provide clean air. We believe more trees should be planted than cut down. If we choose the locations well and care for them properly, our municipality will become greener for everyone.

239451.explanation: Many people find a fireplace cozy. But wood smoke is unhealthy and can cause much nuisance. We would support allowing wood burning only when the weather permits. This can be checked via the Stookwijzer app. This keeps it enjoyable for everyone.

239453.explanation: Many residents enjoy watching fireworks. But fireworks cause nuisance through fine dust and noise for humans and animals. A central fireworks show is good as a transition period with the ultimate goal being a drone or laser show. This way, we can continue celebrating New Year's Eve in a fun and safe way together.

239456.explanation: The library in the center of Bussum must be preserved and housed in a beautiful, future-proof building. This can be done together with other parties, also from the cultural sector. However, the function of a modern library must remain central and not subordinate to a hotspot.

239459.explanation: Camera surveillance is an expensive and heavy measure that infringes on privacy. We only find camera surveillance justifiable in some cases where there is a clear reason and the surveillance is temporary (such as nuisance at the fair).

239460.explanation: Traffic nuisance is a problem, but only having fatbikers checked for their behavior is not allowed and is unfair. Police must be able to address all road users (cars, motorcycles, bicycles, fatbikes, pedestrians) when there are traffic problems.

239462.explanation: We believe that all people working in our municipality with essential professions should be given as much priority as possible for social rental housing in Gooise Meren. In addition to the professions listed here, we also think of public order officers, home care workers, teaching assistants and waste collectors.

239463.explanation: There are currently too few social housing units while demand is high. The current agreement is that 33% of each new plan should be social rental housing. But this does not reduce the shortage fast enough. Therefore, we believe at least 40% of the homes in every plan should be social rental.

239464.explanation: We help people on welfare to participate. The Participation Act should support and empower people. Engaging in social activities is valuable but not mandatory. First, good guidance, attention to health and suitable work. This way, everyone gets a fair chance.

239466.explanation: It is good to ban heavily polluting vehicles from our municipality. These vehicles cause large emissions of nitrogen dioxide and fine particulate matter, which are harmful to our health. Environmental zones encourage residents and businesses to switch to cleaner vehicles, shared mobility or bicycles.

239468.explanation: Permit parking could be a solution in the future to reduce parking pressure in Naarden-Vesting. Currently, there is insufficient support for it. We find it essential to continue dialogue with residents and entrepreneurs about a car-reduced Vesting.

239469.explanation: A maximum speed of 30 km/h reduces traffic noise and thus creates a more pleasant living environment. It also lowers the risk and especially the severity of accidents. Traffic becomes safer for cyclists and pedestrians.

239470.explanation: A district heating network is the cheapest and fairest way for Muiderberg to move away from gas. For many residents, an individual solution with a heat pump is too expensive. A collective district heating network, owned by residents through a local energy cooperative, ensures everyone can participate.

239471.explanation: An animal enclosure is a nice place for recreation, but it must also be good for the animals. Deer are very unsuitable for enclosures. They are classified in the highest risk category for welfare when kept in captivity. Therefore, we want enclosures without deer.

239472.explanation: We already have small-scale wind turbines, like on the island of Pampus. It should be possible for companies and farmers to place small wind turbines on their premises. As a municipality, we want to be able to add wind turbines, for example at our borders near where neighboring municipalities already have them.

239473.explanation: Noise nuisance in the Naarderbos and Keverdijk area is too high. Multiple objectives can be achieved here: stronger noise protection, energy generation and nature enhancement. The municipality must first investigate the options. Then the council can make a well-considered decision.

239474.explanation: At present, there are no parcel pickup points at private homes in residential neighborhoods, so there is no current problem. There are parcel pickup points at existing stores in residential areas, which reduces traffic movements and causes almost no additional nuisance during shopping hours.

239476.explanation: Aircraft noise causes much nuisance for our residents. We support reducing flights, not only over Gooise Meren but also over the other neighboring municipalities of Schiphol. We must work together to make things more pleasant for everyone.

239479.explanation: All children may attend preschool for free. Toddlers with a language delay should also have access to special places at daycare. This way, they learn Dutch faster, grow up with other children and get a fair, equal start. The municipality must cooperate to ensure enough places.

239480.explanation: Muiden needs an inner harbor for small sailing and motorboats. Naarden does not need a separate harbor, but the municipality should improve existing mooring and docking spots.

239481.explanation: The vision for Bussum-South has been adopted. We are now exploring whether homes can be built near the train station. Building in the Vijverpark is not a goal in itself. If building occurs, the area must remain at least as green as now and pleasant for current residents.

239482.explanation: The waiting time for social housing is 8–12 years, which is far too long. We must especially build affordable homes for singles and families. Seniors also often want to downsize. Therefore, we emphasize more social rental and mid-range rental homes.

239483.explanation: The housing shortage is high, but we never build in nature areas. We build first in villages and already developed locations. Agricultural land can only be used if farmers suffer no damage and nature and landscape remain preserved.

239484.explanation: An animal can greatly contribute to the owner’s well-being. An animal should never suffer due to financial problems. Therefore, we believe residents earning up to 130% of the minimum wage should be able to seek support to pay veterinary bills.

239486.explanation: Underground parking garages are planned in Bussum in the coming years, such as near Veldweg. This creates more space in public areas for cycling, walking, and greenery. Unfortunately, underground garages are very expensive. Each project requires weighing costs and benefits.

239487.explanation: The municipality is obliged under the Social Support Act (Wmo) to assist residents who need it to live independently and participate in society. We want to organize this support well and efficiently, preferably without waiting times. We cannot do this with less funding.

239488.explanation: In Gooise Meren, all four centers already have Support and Information Points (STIPs) where mainly older residents come with questions about welfare and health. We want to make the STIPs even more accessible and better known to families with parenting questions, youths with growing-up questions, and residents with financial concerns.

239489.explanation: Everyone should have access to good healthcare close to home. That is why we want to work together with healthcare providers to ensure that general practitioners, physiotherapists, and other healthcare professionals are reachable and accessible in every neighborhood.

239491.explanation: Events are important for the connection between residents and a means to keep our municipality socially, economically, and culturally strong. Rules are of course necessary to keep the events safe and to limit nuisance. Where possible, we will simplify the rules.

239492.explanation: Our beautiful tourist attractions such as Muiderslot Castle and Naarden Fortress are already well capable of attracting day tourists. Our green and watery environment is also popular with walkers, cyclists, and boaters. Additional municipal subsidies for this are not needed.

239437.explanation: The CDA considers the opinion of young people extremely important. However, the instrument of a youth council is not the method the CDA chooses. The CDA is eager to engage in dialogue with young people to determine a good platform for their voices to be heard.

239439.explanation: Reporting via an app is fast, modern, and accessible and offers more possibilities than reporting via the website. The main difference is that through an app you are in direct contact with the municipality.

239441.explanation: The CDA believes that the municipality of Gooise Meren must handle its heritage more carefully. Too many defining and historical buildings have already been demolished in the history of Gooise Meren.

239443.explanation: We choose fair and smart parking policies throughout Gooise Meren.

239444.explanation: That possibility must be offered. To prevent arbitrariness from the municipality, it must be clear in advance what 'important topics' are. This could possibly be identified through a survey among residents.

239445.explanation: However, if it is necessary to comply with legal obligations, the property tax (ozb) may rise by a maximum equal to the inflation adjustment.

239446.explanation: But only on the condition that the fees flow directly back to the events themselves. Additionally, the CDA is committed to simplifying and thus speeding up procedures.

239447.explanation: The CDA believes that everyone with an urgent and distressing housing situation should have the same chance. For example, single parents who have lost their home after a divorce, families forced to sleep in their car, a 35-year-old still living with their parents, or a person with refugee status.

239448.explanation: The CDA focuses on the integration of newcomers with special attention to strengthening digital skills and language proficiency.

239449.explanation: As long as the Distribution Act is in effect, we have the legal obligation to implement it here. We do, however, consider it important that local residents are involved from the start to ensure their interests are properly heard and safeguarded.

239450.explanation: The CDA attaches great importance to livability and amenities for the residents of Gooise Meren. Green spaces are an important facility. For every tree cut down in public areas, there is a replanting obligation.

239451.explanation: The CDA does not want a ban for existing situations. However, we want to enforce regulations for reports of extreme nuisance. Additionally, the CDA is cautious about fireplaces and wood stoves in new construction projects.

239453.explanation: In the interest of safety and community spirit, the CDA wants to set up a pilot to see whether this leads to fewer problems and more enjoyment.

239456.explanation: We see the added value of a cultural hotspot where culture, social interaction, and development come together. This can strengthen the center and connect residents. But we are also realistic and financially responsible, as we must make tough choices in healthcare, social security, and basic services.

239459.explanation: Camera surveillance can contribute to increased safety and the prevention of crime and nuisance. It is not an end in itself but a targeted tool, deployable in places where problems are demonstrably present. It complements the efforts of enforcement officers and police and contributes to a safe and liveable municipality.

239460.explanation: Fat bikes increasingly cause unsafe situations, especially in residential streets, bike paths, and around schools. Therefore, we believe enforcement of behavior is necessary to improve safety. Targeted checks and visible enforcement help strengthen awareness of norms and prevent accidents.

239462.explanation: Although we acknowledge that teachers, nurses, and police officers are indispensable for our society, there is such pressure on the housing market that we believe it is better to look at urgency rather than target groups.

239463.explanation: The housing shortage is significant, especially for people with low or middle incomes. With 21% social rental housing in Gooise Meren, the share is currently too low. 40% social rental housing in new construction is necessary to restore balance, promote flow, and thus give young people, families, and seniors perspective in Gooise Meren.

239464.explanation: We believe that people on social assistance want to participate, but they are not helped by an obligation that can feel like coercion. A social assistance benefit is a safety net, not a contract for counterperformance. What does work is support and guidance towards (volunteer) work or education.

239466.explanation: A healthy living environment is not a luxury but a basic requirement. We see environmental zones as a targeted and careful instrument to reduce air pollution and thus contribute to the health of residents, especially children, the elderly, and people with respiratory problems.

239468.explanation: Naarden Fortress: high parking pressure and little space. Permit parking can help give priority to residents and entrepreneurs and improve livability. But visitors must remain welcome. This requires affordable parking spaces on the edges of the Fortress and attention to (care) visitors.

239469.explanation: Traffic safety is important, but a generic 30 km/h rule throughout the entire built-up area does not work everywhere. We choose tailor-made solutions per road: 30 km/h in residential streets and recreational areas where safety is paramount; 50 km/h on roads with a traffic flow function, provided they are safely designed.

239470.explanation: This project makes a major contribution to the energy transition in Gooise Meren. Provided there is support among residents and it is financially responsible for both the energy company and the consumers (residents).

239471.explanation: The council has adopted the 'Animal-Friendly Petting Farms' quality mark that makes the arrival of deer impossible.

239472.explanation: There is no room for this in Gooise Meren. No homes can be built around wind turbines. The CDA considers building homes more important than wind turbines.

239473.explanation: The CDA specifically supports two additional solar noise barriers near Keverdijk and Muiderberg.

239474.explanation: Parcel pickup points have become indispensable and are now part of society. Especially for the elderly and people with mobility issues, it is important that they can pick up their parcels close to home. However, the municipality must enforce against possible nuisance.

239476.explanation: We take noise nuisance seriously, but regulating air traffic and night flights is primarily a national government responsibility. Locally, we focus on measures that do have an effect, such as home insulation, spatial planning, and supporting residents who experience inconvenience.

239479.explanation: Every child deserves a fair start. If a toddler has a (language/developmental) delay, money should never be a barrier to getting help. That is why we support free preschool programs. It increases the chance that children will do well later in primary school.

239480.explanation: Inner harbors for small boats strengthen historic and watery cores. They increase livability, support local tourism, and provide residents better access to the water without large-scale crowds. Implementation must be financially responsible, preferably phased.

239481.explanation: Vijverpark is an important place for meeting, relaxation, and livability in the neighborhood. Therefore, we are against housing on this site unless strict conditions are demonstrably met: support from local residents, appropriate building only, financially responsible, and strengthening greenery.

239482.explanation: Mid-range housing is needed, but the biggest shortages are in social rental and affordable homes. That is the priority. We choose balance: first space for social rental and truly affordable homes; then mid-range rental and homeownership where the location and need are suitable.

239483.explanation: Agricultural land is scarce and valuable for the landscape, food, and the character of Gooise Meren. That is why we are against building on agricultural land. We choose smart building within existing areas: restructuring and expansion. This way, we preserve the balance between housing, nature, and agriculture.

239484.explanation: Reimbursing veterinary costs is not part of the municipality's core tasks, but we support people in financial need and are aware of distressing situations. We focus on education and prevention, such as the importance of pet insurance and responsible pet ownership.

239486.explanation: Underground parking garages can help free up surface space for housing, greenery, and quality of stay in Bussum. Therefore, we are basically in favor. However, we set clear conditions. Underground parking is expensive and must not lead to unaffordable housing construction.

239487.explanation: The Social Support Act (Wmo) is a basic provision for residents who need support to continue living independently and participate in society. Cuts here directly affect vulnerable residents and are unacceptable. Good Wmo support often prevents heavier and more expensive care later.

239488.explanation: Care must be close and understandable. Residents should not get lost in rules, forms, and referrals. We support one care desk per center where residents can go with their questions. A low-threshold contact point that thinks along and helps find appropriate support.

239489.explanation: Good care nearby is important, but not every neighborhood needs its own health center. We opt for a tailored approach: health centers in places with high demand for care; in other neighborhoods, good accessibility to care through existing practices, district nursing, and local collaboration.

239491.explanation: Events bring life to the communities, connect residents, and strengthen associations and entrepreneurship. For us, the biggest gain lies in simplifying the permit process: fewer rules and paperwork, clear procedures; one point of contact at the municipality.

239492.explanation: Spending extra money on a tourism campaign is not a priority, especially in times when we must make tough choices in care, housing, and livability. More tourists do not automatically mean higher quality. In some communities, extra crowds actually cause nuisance and parking pressure.

239437.explanation: We believe young people should have influence over topics that are important to them now and in the future. Topics such as affordable housing, climate, nightlife, health, and work. We want to give young people a formal advisory role toward the council.

239439.explanation: Residents can already report public space problems like loose pavement tiles, litter, or broken streetlights online to the municipality. Developing an additional app would only cost more money.

239441.explanation: As a sustainability party, we strongly support preserving and renovating homes and buildings that are in good condition and have historical value. We want to preserve important heritage such as the Huibert van Eijkenstraat. This also from an environmental perspective (resources, CO₂ emissions, etc.).

239443.explanation: We want cheap parking permits for village residents and visitors of residents. To counter parking pressure from outsiders (e.g., beach visitors), paid parking must be introduced. This should apply throughout Gooise Meren.

239444.explanation: There are drawbacks to referenda. Turnout is often low. Complex subjects are often oversimplified. It delays decision-making. Organizing a referendum also costs a lot of money and time. We believe there are other ways to involve residents in thinking along.

239445.explanation: Through local taxes, residents contribute to the well-being, livability, and amenities in our municipality. Therefore, we consider it acceptable, provided the money is demonstrably spent on facilities that directly affect residents and there are exemptions for residents with a low income.

239446.explanation: Ensuring events run safely and orderly involves costs (for the municipality). To do this, sufficient coverage must be available.

239447.explanation: This is a statutory task of the municipality.

239448.explanation: Provided this yields demonstrable results. Good integration of newcomers is important for all residents of Gooise Meren.

239449.explanation: As a municipality, we must comply with national legislation. At present, every municipality has a legal obligation to arrange shelter.

239450.explanation: The Party for Environment and Climate is a strong supporter of this. In our election program, we have a plan to plant 60,000 trees (about 5,000 per year) because trees provide many climate and health benefits, such as cooling, biodiversity, cleaner air, and reduced stress.

239451.explanation: Wood stoves and fireplaces produce wood smoke containing a lot of fine particles and harmful substances. This causes respiratory complaints, heart, and lung diseases. Therefore, the municipality must reduce wood smoke emissions. We are a participant in the Clean Air Agreement for healthier air.

239453.explanation: We are in favor of a fireworks-free municipality. Alternative shows can be offered, such as light shows and music.

239456.explanation: First, we believe the area vision for Bussum center must be well executed before plans are made for a cultural hotspot. We want to facilitate this if there is demand for a cultural hotspot but not force it. The most important thing is that we get a beautiful, sustainable library.

239459.explanation: In principle, we oppose surveillance cameras throughout the municipality, but we recognize that they can contribute to a sense of security in certain problem areas. We are thinking here of nightlife areas and traffic unsafe places. But this never guarantees complete safety.

239460.explanation: PLEK is not a big supporter of fatbikes due to nuisance and danger to other cyclists and pedestrians.

239462.explanation: Of course. We believe that people with essential professions should be able to live close to their workplace.

239463.explanation: We want the total percentage of affordable housing to be increased to 60% of all new construction: 40% social rental housing and 20% affordable owner-occupied homes. Research shows the demand for affordable housing is large. The demand for expensive homes is only 10%.

239464.explanation: Only on a voluntary basis. Motivating people instead of forcing. We prefer positive encouragement over coercion.

239466.explanation: The Party for Environment and Climate (the name says it all) supports measures to improve air quality. Zero emission zones help with this. At the same time, they also contribute to achieving climate goals. See our election program for further explanation.

239468.explanation: We want to introduce permit parking throughout the municipality to encourage car-free areas. The possibility for tourists and visitors to park at the outskirts of the city should remain.

239469.explanation: This promotes safety for cyclists and pedestrians, helps reduce traffic noise nuisance, and decreases emissions including fine particles.

239470.explanation: There are still too many uncertainties to make a concrete statement. If this eventually provides an affordable and sustainable heating supply for residents, then PLEK supports it.

239471.explanation: Due to a motion from the Party for Environment and Climate, petting farms must now carry the 'Animal-Friendly Petting Farm' certification. Deer are wild animals that need space and tranquility. At a petting farm, they experience stress from human contact and limited living space.

239472.explanation: Placement of some wind turbines, if this is possible at a healthy and safe distance from homes and nature reserves. This can generate electricity for thousands of households. This is much more efficient use of space than the tens of hectares of solar meadows needed to generate the same amount of energy.

239473.explanation: From day one, the Party for Environment and Climate has opposed this nonsensical project and was previously the only one to vote against it. We oppose mass tree felling and nature destruction for the construction of a solar noise barrier and have advocated for years for the free noise barrier offered by Rijkswaterstaat.

239474.explanation: Homes are not business premises. This also causes parking nuisance.

239476.explanation: PLEK, Party for Environment and Climate, strongly supports this. Noise pollution causes stress and health complaints. Airplanes also emit many pollutants.

239479.explanation: Definitely. This pays off and leads to lower costs later in life and less dropout or school leaving. This promotes equal opportunities.

239480.explanation: PLEK opposes this because there are already more than enough facilities for small recreational boats. We also do not want to damage the protected cityscape. Water quality does not benefit from many motorized boats.

239481.explanation: We absolutely do not want to allow housing construction in city parks. Parks provide peace, greenery, meeting places, cooling, and water retention. The Vijverpark is now less natural, but the municipality can improve the nature value here by planting more native vegetation and making the pond more nature-friendly.

239482.explanation: Mid-priced homes are necessary, but the need for affordable rental and owner-occupied housing is much greater and more urgent. Moreover, we are far behind our own targets for affordable and mid-priced housing. This encourages circulation.

239483.explanation: We want to allow new construction on agricultural land only on a very limited scale if there are no more infill possibilities elsewhere. But not in nature reserves, forests, and parks.

239486.explanation: These cost millions of euros per garage. Recently, it appeared that the construction of an underground version of the Olmengarage would cost 22 million euros. That was ultimately cancelled. We advocate for a limited number of parking garages. More garages lead to more car use. We want car-free centers.

239487.explanation: The Party for Environment and Climate believes that personal income should be taken into account. It must not be the case that people with low incomes are disadvantaged by Wmo use by higher income groups.

239488.explanation: One central desk seems sufficient to us.

239489.explanation: It is especially important for the elderly that healthcare is nearby and easily accessible.

239492.explanation: Because we already benefit for free from the promotional activities of the municipality of Amsterdam. They promote Muiderslot (Amsterdam Castle) and Naarden-Vesting.

239437.explanation: Opinion is fine, but not always participating, because the youth council sets its own agenda.

239441.explanation: Pre-war houses and buildings are mostly located in neighborhoods with protected status and therefore may not be demolished.

239443.explanation: There is currently no reason to change free parking.

239445.explanation: Hart Voor Gooise Meren wants costs for residents not to increase. This also includes waste and sewer charges.

239446.explanation: Hart Voor Gooise Meren believes that events bring residents together/connect them. Therefore, we agree with the statement and think it should be free.

239447.explanation: Every priority limits the chance for others. There must be consideration for fairer/better distribution of housing. The priority scheme currently limits proper allocation. So not one person in a whole house just because they have priority.

239448.explanation: No more money, because the current effort is sufficient.

239450.explanation: As many as possible, but not necessarily 500.

239451.explanation: Fireplaces are allowed under the right and permitted conditions, but combustion stoves that heat a whole house should be banned.

239453.explanation: Firework shows can also be a New Year's Eve event.

239459.explanation: Under strict (privacy) conditions to combat crime and vandalism.

239460.explanation: Hart Voor Gooise Meren considers traffic safety crucial in our municipality.

239462.explanation: Research has already been done on priority for teachers. This is complex and yields little result. Every priority limits the chance for others, such as craftsmen, etc.

239463.explanation: On own land, Hart builds 100% affordable (social to mid-range). From market parties, we ask for 30-30-30. This means we reach an average of 50% affordable housing. Hart Voor Gooise Meren has achieved this.

239464.explanation: It would be desirable rather than mandatory. Hart finds it important that everyone participates.

239466.explanation: Hart Voor Gooise Meren supports measures that are good for the environment. National policy guides us.

239468.explanation: This would be the final blow to the local economy, tourism, and museum visitors, because we would then exclude 'our guests'.

239469.explanation: Hart Voor Gooise Meren is willing to look at the larger roads with a regional function.

239470.explanation: Research as soon as possible into nuclear energy 2.0 as an energy source to, among other things, heat houses. (Mini nuclear plants based on thorium).

239471.explanation: They may also be other animals, such as alpacas.

239472.explanation: Hopefully this means 'wind turbines', because we could allow windmills under conditions regarding height. Research into nuclear energy 2.0 as soon as possible.

239473.explanation: The process is ongoing; in consultation with the neighborhood, Hart does not exclude alternatives. Hart also wants soundproofing measures at Thijssepark.

239474.explanation: We find a residents' initiative fine.

239476.explanation: Gooise Meren cooperates with many parties to minimize nuisance.

239479.explanation: Hart Voor Gooise Meren considers testing toddlers for developmental delays putting the cart before the horse.

239480.explanation: Well anchored locally. The resident comes first.

239481.explanation: Our iconic pond with large fountain, besides the water tower, is a landmark for many residents. The park and surroundings themselves can be optimized, including possibilities for housing development.

239482.explanation: We assume this also concerns affordable owner-occupied homes.

239483.explanation: Our open polder landscape must be cherished, however, Hart Voor Gooise Meren wants to enable our farmers to sustainably further develop their farms and the landscape for the future.

239484.explanation: Residents who need a pet for social or societal reasons may, under conditions yet to be determined, possibly qualify for compensation.

239486.explanation: Hart Voor Gooise Meren has advocated for years for an underground parking garage under Wilhelminaplantsoen and expansion of the Scapino garage.

239488.explanation: At least one counter. The resident comes first!

239489.explanation: Not in every neighborhood, but nearby.

239491.explanation: Events are crucial for social cohesion. The organizations that generally do this voluntarily must be optimally supported and appreciated. Especially in terms of permitting!

239492.explanation: The new promotion organization deserves our support, also financially, to realize their plans that will attract quality tourists to visit our wonderful municipality.

statements.title: Pre-war houses and buildings in Gooise Meren must not be demolished.
A new reception site for asylum seekers must be established in Gooise Meren if the temporary emergency shelter in Naarden closes.

titleParts.text: In Gooise Meren moet een nieuwe opvanglocatie voor

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.
A building for urgent and temporary shelter for people who need accommodation, for example asylum seekers.

statements.title: The municipality should install CCTV surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The Warmtenet project in Muiderberg must go ahead.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Gooise Meren should introduce environmental zones to keep polluting vehicles out of the town centers.

titleParts.glossary: An area where very polluting cars are not allowed to drive.

titleParts.text: invoeren om vervuilende voertuigen uit de centra te weren.

statements.title: The residents of Gooise Meren should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Gooise Meren moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: At least 40% of newly built homes in Gooise Meren should be social rental housing. Currently 21% of homes in Gooise Meren are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Gooise Meren moet minimaal 40% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Gooise Meren is nu 21% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Permit parking should be introduced in Naarden-Vesting. Only residents and business owners from Naarden-Vesting would then be allowed to park there.

titleParts.glossary: A permit to park in a certain place. You have to pay for that right.

titleParts.text: komen. Alleen bewoners en ondernemers uit Naarden-Vesting kunnen er dan parkeren.

statements.title: Parcel collection points should not be located next to houses in residential neighborhoods.
Building should be permitted on agricultural land within the municipality.
Residents who cannot pay their veterinary bills should receive financial assistance from the municipality.
More underground parking garages should be built in Bussum.
The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Newcomers learn the language and customs of the Netherlands.
People who are new to the Netherlands.

statements.title: Property tax (ozb) must not be increased.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: Every neighborhood in the municipality should have a health center.

statements.theme: Priority housing for teachers, nurses, and police officers
statements.title: When allocating social rental housing, teachers, nurses and police officers should be given priority wherever possible.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten leraren, verpleegkundigen en politiemensen zoveel mogelijk voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: New construction in Gooise Meren should focus on mid-priced rental and owner-occupied homes.
The fees for municipal event permits should be reduced.

titleParts.text: De prijzen van gemeentelijke vergunningen voor

titleParts.glossary: A temporary, organized gathering. For example a street party, a flea market, or a music festival.

statements.title: The deer parks should be reinstated in Naarden and Bussum.
People who have been granted asylum should no longer be given priority for social rental housing in the municipality.

titleParts.text: Statushouders moeten geen voorrang meer krijgen op

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality must organize a fireworks show every year on New Year’s Eve.
Parking in Muiderberg must remain free.
The municipality should invest in a campaign to attract more tourists to Gooise Meren.
Wind turbines should be installed in Gooise Meren.

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

shootoutStatements.title: The maximum speed limit within built-up areas must be reduced to 30 km/h across the entire municipality.
Gooise Meren must put more pressure on the national government to reduce air traffic and night flights over the municipality.
In the centre of Bussum there must be a cultural hotspot by the library.
The municipality should spend less money on social support for residents (Wmo).
New housing should be built on the site of the Vijverpark.
Toddlers with a developmental delay should be able to attend a free program before they start primary school.
The municipality should abandon plans for a solar noise barrier along the A1 in Naarden.
The municipality should plant at least 500 new trees every year.
Gooise Meren should set up a youth council for 12- to 18-year-olds that is always allowed to have a say in municipal plans.
Muiden and Naarden should quickly get inner harbors for small boats.
The municipality should urge the police to check the behavior of fatbike riders more often.
The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain allowed in Gooise Meren.
In each population center in Gooise Meren there should be one service desk where residents can go with care-related questions.
The municipality should make it easier to organize events.
Residents of Gooise Meren should be able to report problems in public spaces via a reporting app on their phone.
